explicit_text: My family moved to Mae La Oon refugee camp in Thailand after escaping the war in Burma. Life in Mae La Oon was easier than in Burma because we didn’t have to work the farms every morning, and we received free food through notes given by camp workers. The amount of food depended on the size of our family, but if we wanted more, we had to work to earn money. It was at Mae La Oon that we started the process to resettle in the United States, including interviews and health checks in Mae Sot.
implicit_text: My family moved to a refugee camp in western Thailand after escaping the war in Burma. Life in that camp was easier than in Burma because we didn’t have to work the farms every morning, and we received free food through notes given by camp workers. The amount of food depended on the size of our family, but if we wanted more, we had to work to earn money. It was at this camp that we started the process to resettle in the United States, including interviews and health checks in a nearby border town.

explicit_text: Thailand was the country where my family lived in the Mae La Oon refugee camp after fleeing Burma. We stayed there for many years, receiving food aid and starting to rebuild our lives. It was also in Thailand, specifically in Mae Sot, where we went through interviews and health screenings before moving to the United States. Life in Thailand was better than in Burma, but still difficult because we lacked freedom and enough food.
implicit_text: This Southeast Asian country was where my family lived in the Mae La Oon refugee camp after fleeing Burma. We stayed there for many years, receiving food aid and starting to rebuild our lives. Life here was better than in Burma, but still difficult because we lacked freedom and enough food.

explicit_text: My family resettled in the United States after spending many years as refugees in Thailand. We arrived after going through interviews and health checks in Mae Sot, around 2017. Life in the United States has been much better; we have access to the food we want and free education. America gave us freedom and a fresh start, which we are very thankful for, especially after the hardships we faced in Burma and Thailand.
implicit_text: My family resettled in North America after spending many years as refugees in Thailand. We arrived after going through interviews and health checks in Mae Sot, around 2017. Life here has been much better; we have access to the food we want and free education. explicit_text: Burma was where my family originally lived before the war forced us to flee. We had a hard life there, farming to grow food but never having enough to eat. When the war started, Burmese soldiers killed many Karen people and destroyed homes and farms to take over the land. To survive, my family sold our crops to get money before escaping to Thailand. The fear of Burmese soldiers was constant, and escaping was the only way to stay alive.
implicit_text: This was where my family originally lived before the war forced us to flee. We had a hard life there, farming to grow food but never having enough to eat. When the war started, soldiers from the ruling government killed many Karen people and destroyed homes and farms to take over the land. To survive, my family sold our crops to get money before escaping to Thailand. The fear of these soldiers was constant, and escaping was the only way to stay alive.

explicit_text: Mae Sot is the place in Thailand where my family went for interviews and health checks before moving to the United States. This step was part of the resettlement process that happened about a year after we applied to go to America. Mae Sot was important because it helped us prepare for our new life abroad. Although we had already lived in Mae La Oon camp, Mae Sot was where the official screenings took place.
